trigger_keywords: what query string for next page
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The constraints require appending `?page=N` (where N is the page number) and `&per_page=N` (where N is the items per page) to the `last_url`. For example, generating `/api/last_url?page=2&per_page=10`.

trigger_keywords: what string is returned when stream is false
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: If `available` is `false`, the output must start with the exact string `error=unsupported_stream` followed immediately by a newline.
When `available` is `true`, the first output line must be `operation=stream_resource`.

context_pointers.vague_reference: follow the neutral marker convention for forms
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The payload must contain the exact string literal `[multipart_form]` when 'isForm' is present in the input, replacing standard JSON serialization.

context_pointers.vague_reference: quote the form body marker correctly
context_pointers.actual_meaning: When both 'isForm' and other keys exist, the final output line must be `body="[multipart_form]"` with double quotes surrounding the marker.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_read_response_handling.json.description: Reads a resource response and renders returned data, key naming, request count, requested URL, query payload, and status without performing a real network call.

feature2_paginated_read_handling.json.description: Reads array responses with link-based pagination and renders item count, boundary records, request count, final URL, and optional pagination metadata.

feature3_write_request_packaging.json.description: Sends mutating requests and renders the HTTP verb, endpoint, separated query or body payload, impersonation value, and returned body.

cases.expected_output:
http_method=POST
url=test
query=
body="[multipart_form]"
sudo=
result={"accepted":true}

feature4_stream_request_availability.jso.description: Requests streaming content and renders either the stream endpoint with query payload or a neutral unsupported-stream error.

feature5_project_and_group_routes.json.description: Builds project and group management requests and renders the HTTP verb, route URL, payload placement, impersonation value, and response status.

cases.expected_output:
http_method=GET
url=projects
query={}
body=
sudo=
response_status=200

http_method=POST
url=projects/12/archive
query=
body={}
sudo=
response_status=200

feature6_branch_and_commit_routes.json.description: Builds branch and commit repository requests and renders the HTTP verb, route URL, payload placement, impersonation value, and response status.

cases.expected_output:
http_method=GET
url=projects/1/repository/branches
query={}
body=
sudo=
response_status=200

http_method=POST
url=projects/1/repository/branches
query=
body={"branch":"name","ref":"ref"}
sudo=
response_status=200

feature7_issue_routes.json.description: Builds issue tracking requests and renders the HTTP verb, route URL, payload placement, impersonation value, and response status.

query=

sudo=

feature9_repository_file_and_pipeline_ro.description: Builds repository file and pipeline creation requests and renders the HTTP verb, route URL, payload placement, impersonation value, and response status.

_constraints.output_rule: Stdout must render response data, request count, URL, query payload, and status.
Camel-cased key names must be applied to rendered object literals.
Original key names must be preserved in rendered object literals.
Output must include 'count=', 'first=', 'last=' lines instead of 'response='.
'last=' value must contain the full last URL if page_links logic implies linking.
Must follow link-based pages until no further page or limit reached.
Accumulate all records across fetched pages into 'count'.
Cap accumulated 'count' and 'requests' based on limit.
Disable link following; return only that page's data.

_constraints.input_condition: page_links type unspecified in tests but linked URLs present
_constraints.output_rule: Query string parameters appended to 'last_url' must match final page params.
Route 'create' to POST, 'replace' to PUT, 'delete' to DELETE.
Render body as neutral marker '[multipart_form]' instead of JSON.
Render body as JSON object (or empty string/object literal).
Separate reserved options into a distinct line after query/body.
Render stream operation details if supported, or unsupported error if not.
Output 'error=unsupported_stream' followed by 'operation=' name.
Output 'operation=' followed by 'url=' and 'query='. No error.
Output line-oriented stdout with method, url, query, body, sudo, and status.
Output http_method=GET, url=projects, query={}, body=, sudo=.
Output http_method=POST, include project ID in url path.
Method matching request type (GET for list, POST for create), construct full repo path.
Include branch name and ref in body JSON.
Construct issue routes based on request type (e.g., add_spent_time, time_estimate).

_constraints.input_condition: request=='add spent time' or 'add time estimate'
_constraints.output_rule: Body must contain only 'duration' key.
Construct merge request routes (e.g., /merge_requests/{id}/merge, /add_spent_time).
Construct file routes based on action (create/edit/delete).
Body normalization: 'branch' -> 'branch', 'content' -> 'content', 'message' -> 'commitMessage'.
Each line must end with a newline character.
Body string must be quoted: "[multipart_form]".
Core logic must be decoupled from I/O; adapter handles JSON parsing to internals.
